_comment: kinoheld IDs discovered 2026-07-07 via the cinemas(search:) GraphQL query (API capture in scraper/debug/woki_curl.txt); every ID verified against programByMovie. 'website' is the cinema's own site — showtime links point there instead of kinoheld; non-kinoheld ticket deeplinks (CineStar, kinotickets.express, chain shops) are kept. Cinemas with 'source': 'custom' have their scraper in sources/custom.py.

cinemas.name: Kinopolis Bad Godesberg
cinemas._note: listed as 'Kinopolis Bonn' on kinoheld, Moltkestr. 7 = Bad Godesberg; their data carries no OV/OmU flags, so everything classifies as DE

cinemas.name: Bonner Kinemathek
cinemas._note: listed as 'Bonner Kinemathek - Kino in der Brotfabrik' on kinoheld

cinemas.name: Stern Lichtspiele
cinemas._note: = CineStar Bonn (Markt 8), listed as 'CineStar Bonn' on kinoheld; keeps its webticketing3.cinestar.de deeplinks

cinemas.name: Metropolis
cinemas._note: mostly-OV house. Not usably on kinoheld (id 974 there is an event venue) — scraped from their CineWeb site, tickets via kinotickets.express. Unmarked foreign-language films are corrected DE->OmU in main.py (fix_metropolis_ov)

cinemas.name: Filmpalette
cinemas._note: their https is broken (cert), http works. kinoheld drops their OmU/OmeU markers — corrected from their homepage text in main.py (apply_filmpalette_languages)

cinemas.name: Rex am Ring
cinemas._note: also kinoheld id 1088, but kinoheld drops nearly all their OmU/OV markers — their own CineWeb site is the truthful source (and has exact kinotickets.express links)

cinemas.name: Cinedom
cinemas._note: IS on kinoheld after all — no custom scraper needed
